item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Doesn't the AWCY hub link sort of ruin the intent of that passage a bit? I believe the AWCY references are intentionally supposed to be sly in the article (that being the only real more obvious one), so the link might be jarring in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OMG I didn't realize that until I read this comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The gecko footnote made me smile smugly. Enjoyed this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Mrs" = autocorrect for "mess".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To answer the first part, the bottlecap is basically them screwing with the Foundation ala "YOU GET IT, RIGHT? LOLOLOL".

No clue what that second part is asking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone explain the bottle cap? Is that just to Mrs with the found?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Time Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

While talking with Silber about possible links for SCP-1502 it was mentioned that 1502 and 1802 have shared origins, hence why the Wikiwalk added a link from 1502 to 1802. Now it is the time to repay the favor. Add in a link to the AWCY? hub (it is an AWCY? skip after all) and there you go. I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1802 is an artificial humanoid measuring approximately 30 cm in height. Its body has been crudely assembled from lacquered chicken bones, scraps of iron and leather, wire, heavy twine, and a tin can which serves as the creature's head. The object wears a square of white canvas fashioned into a semblance of a lab coat, and has safety goggles secured on its "head" using screws. SCP-1802 walks with an unsteady gait, but is manually dexterous and capable of using tools.

To this:

SCP-1802 is an artificial humanoid measuring approximately 30 cm in height. Its body has been crudely assembled from lacquered chicken bones, scraps of iron and leather, wire, heavy twine, and a tin can which serves as the creature's head1. The object wears a square of white canvas fashioned into a semblance of a lab coat, and has safety goggles secured on its "head" using screws. SCP-1802 walks with an unsteady gait, but is manually dexterous and capable of using tools.

Changing this:

SCP-1802: I look around. I keep anything I do not understand and study it to learn. By this task I become cool.

To this:

SCP-1802: I look around. I keep anything I do not understand and study it to learn. By this task I become cool.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarity in construction materials between SCP-1802 and SCP-1502 suggests a shared origin.

item.title: Re: Hey Look, it's a SCP being us.
item.content:encoded:
what do you think the line "By this task I become cool" means?

It means Skip is not cool yet.

item.title: Hey Look, it's a SCP being us.
item.content:encoded:
I didn't think about it, until it started containing things (like a lizard). This is a self-parody of our Foundation, and a kinda cute one at that.

You get +1.

Also, did someone from AWCY make it? Just curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instant upvote at mini-682 containment breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how they found a perfectly normal bottlecap that was probably just left because nobody noticed it, decided it was anomalous, and put it in containment….

EXACTLY like Skip and his collection of completely unremarkable garbage.

And they don't even notice. It's really kinda glorious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? This is one of the least "tacked-on" references in any AWCY skip in my eyes. I didn't even get it until I saw the tags, and then I had to figure out what about this was AWCY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this guy is really cute and i'd upvote if it wasnt for the obviously tacked on awcy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone just removed the object subclasses without my permission.

The subclasses were something I experimented with in a couple of my SCPs months ago, but I decided that they didn't work and removed them from whatever pages of mine that had them. I ended up forgetting. So I won't be reverting this particular unauthorized edit because I had planned on making it anyway.

Of course, the person who made the edit had no way of knowing that. If anyone wants something on one of my pages edited, just talk to me about it before you go changing it yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Object Subclasses: Automaton, Humanoid, Sentient

Isn't that what tags are for?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would love to see an automaton named Katie that destroys random stuff.
Yeah! … Don't really know what else to say…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. leeeeeetle bit of pride at getting the joke before reading comments.
2. HOLY CRAP THAT IS AWESOME. Seriously, this is now my headcannon for the embodiment of AWCY. The mockery is fantastic. I have to gush here. This article is a gorram work of art. You, sir, are, in my book, cool.
I want an SCP article based on that bottlecap!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The whole point of Death of the Author is that the author can't tell you that your interpretation is wrong. So his view is perfectly valid despite your ALL CAPS objections.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nothing to do with dumpsters or rubbishi whatsoever. It's only because of the trend to pronounce "SCP" like "Skip".

If the automaton's name was "Skip" just because it collects rubbish in the way that dumpsters collect rubbish, the AWCYer wouldn't have had any reason to emphasize "WHEN THE SCIENTISTS CAPTURE YOU, YOU MUST TELL THEM THAT YOUR NAME IS 'SKIP', THAT IS A VERY IMPORTANT DETAIL AND YOU MUST REMEMBER IT".

It's hard to do death-of-the-author when the author is right there telling you that you're wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I understand, it's a play on words on "skip", another term for a dumpster. The SCP collects rubbish, and is a SCP (Pronounced skip).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And it implies that the SCPs are just random trash, too …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a parody of the Foundation, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is basically humanity in a nutshell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's not really designed to be a spy. It's a statement that is criticizing the foundation. And art, which is AWCY's real signature, is very often about mocking and criticizing established organizations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: because he alludes to becoming cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get why they would automatically assume it's AWCY?… Doesn't really fit their MO. Since when do them make robot spies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's tagged as AWCY now. Which I sort of appreciate because this is by far my favorite AWCY SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't understand what was going on, I needed to look in the comments :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, given that it made a subtle reference to them, it looks like AWCY? in my headcanon.
And it's the only one that I actually remembered, that counts for something.

Oh, and the not-shark is dangerous, it killed two swimmers before they took it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it because he always skips when he moves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this isn't tagged as AWCY and can't be presumed to be as much. Also, SCP-1043 isn't innately dangerous either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only AWCY? sk-
…
The only AWCY? object that doesn't present a direct danger?

Are you sure?

Plus, it's not necessarily AWCY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god.
This is the ONLY AWCY? SCP that I can remember that doesn't kill, maim, or otherwise present some serious danger to the foundation or anyone else for that matter.

At first, I didn't quite get it. I realized it was AWCY? but I didn't realize the meaning.
Then I read the comments.
I think the Foundation could use some ice for that burn.
This little guy is hilarious, how he tries so hard to understand, and yet he's an effective metaphor for the Foundation itself.

Wait a minute… Skip tries to open up his own head because it's anomalous… what if…
…the O5 team is anomalous as well?
Dun dun DUNNNNNNNN.

item.title: Magic words
item.content:encoded:
When the AWCY guy told Skip "you do not understand much"…

… was that descriptive? Or prescriptive?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read and go all 'aww, is a lil foundation robot!'
Then get in the comments, and notice someone had to -explain- that fact…

._. Really now? seems kinda obvious to me, lil guy is the fundation's perfect pet! XD

item.title: Parallels!
item.content:encoded:
"The gecko escaped SCP-1802's possession shortly after it placed the lizard in storage."

I see what you did there.

item.title: Re: Art! Woo!
item.content:encoded: Skip is clearly aware that its own head is anomalous. So it wants to open up its own head to find out what's inside. Obviously.

item.title: Re: Art! Woo!
item.content:encoded:
Thats adorable, apollyna.

I don't get why there's no attention paid to the fact that it constantly attempts to tear open its own head.

item.title: Art! Woo!
item.content:encoded: I drew him, sorta. I thought I should post it here for posterity.

item.title: Re: +101
item.content:encoded:
Everyone else has already said it on different articles under different titles. It's unique that your "+100" post was pretty much that same message rather than any form of congrats so kudos on that aspect.

Congrats on the +100 mile stone, keep up the good work Silberescher.
Fuck, these noisemakers are broken. I don't think +100 means what it used to. Not that this isn't a good SCP, I'm just saiyan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought it was pretty obvious. The trash robot is to the foundation what the foundation is to whatever actual reality lies beyond what we perceive as the mundane reality.

And it gives pointers to being AWCY without ramming it down our throats, randomly killing people lolwut, or solely being kind of artsy man. And it leaves open the possibility it doesn't have crap to do with AWCY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got the parodyness immediately, but I didn't really follow the bottle cap bit.

I think this is a good use of AWCY?, and I especially liked the gecko bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There may not be a canon, but there is a SOP. And it tends to be followed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got the caricature-ness right from the start, and spent most of the article thinking "this should be a -J". Then it clicked and I realized that it feels like a -J because it is a joke, and it should be on the main list and not the joke list because it is an in-universe joke.

I also thought that it was a little out of character for the staff to ignore the jibe. In my experience, scientists and academics don't respond well to even minor insults to their dignity. But on reading these comments and seeing how many people didn't get it themselves, I'm changing my interpretation. Now I think that the Foundation staff just don't realize that it is a cartoon of themselves. The Foundation missing the point so hard makes it all the funnier.

Minor quibble, why does it get a whole humanoid containment cell? A cell scaled for a full-sized human is a massive waste of space for something only a foot tall. Not to mention the bed and plumbing for something that (presumably) doesn't sleep or eat. It's cute, and I would feel bad if they locked it in a box just big enough, but why not an unused closet somewhere? With occasional random crap for it to "contain" if the Foundation is feeling generous.

PS: While I agree with others that you shouldn't make the AWCY or other references any less subtle in the article, I do think that it should be tagged for it. Mostly because it fits my interpretation of AWCY and their attitude so well and I really want it to be part of that sub-set for anyone who's reading up on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got it as soon as I read the name.

And subsequently upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love this. I was just okay with the concept (kinda 'meh' on it actually), but all the details really make this. The gecko, the road sign, the particular way you did the Are We Cool Yet angle, the 'FIGURE IT OUT' bottlecap, and of course the subtle insult to the Foundation (which I didn't get first read). Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The sign was later found to be anomalous and classified as SCP-329-J.

…Well, it could have been, except the pic for 329-j is a Yield sign, and this one's described as a deer Xing sign. Oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed both the subtle AWCY inference and the almost dumbshow-esque abstraction of the Foundation. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed, when I read that comment I thought he was being an idiot, but all along he simply knew what I didn't…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I share your pain. Even moreso with vezaz's comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I finally 'get it', dear lord, it was so obvious how did I miss it?

+ Damn
- Damn

It's a robot made of junk that collects trash like a pack rat, an obvious parody of the Foundation and it's containment practices.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Waaaait is the lizard a 682 joke?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It had me at the escaping lizard. I kept chuckling at that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, that's what Sites are for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's true. I think it was the combination of the "I do not understand much" line and the fact that after it removed the street sign it couldn't actually move it that made me go d'awwww. He just tries so hard! Such a champ!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was it seriously such an obvious reason?
